Class Taclet

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.prover.rules.Taclet
All Implemented Interfaces:
Named, Rule
Direct Known Subclasses:
Taclet
public abstract class Taclet extends Object implements Rule
A taclet (formerly known as schematic theory specific rule) is a rule representation used within the KeY verification system.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    protected final @NonNull ApplicationRestriction
    applicationRestriction
    The restriction(s) for applying this update.
    protected final Sequent
    assumesSequent
    the assumes sequent of the taclet
    protected @Nullable ImmutableSet<SchemaVariable>
    assumesVariables
    Set of schema variables of the assumes part
    protected @Nullable ImmutableSet<QuantifiableVariable>
    boundVariables
    cache; contains set of all bound variables
    protected final ChoiceExpr
    choices
    the set of taclet options for this taclet
    protected final String
    displayName
    name displayed by the pretty printer

    find
    contains the find term
    protected final ImmutableList<TacletGoalTemplate>
    goalTemplates
    the list of taclet goal descriptions

    matcher
    The taclet matcher
    protected final Name
    name
    unique name of the taclet
    protected final ImmutableMap<@NonNull SchemaVariable, TacletPrefix>
    prefixMap
    map from a schemavariable to its prefix.
    protected final ImmutableList<RuleSet>
    ruleSets
    list of rulesets (formerly known as heuristics) the taclet belongs to
    protected final ImmutableSet<TacletAnnotation>
    tacletAnnotations
    Annotations attached to taclets, specifying their kind, e.g., lemma.

    trigger of the taclet

    Additional generic conditions for schema variable instantiations.

    Variables that have to be created each time the taclet is applied.
    protected final ImmutableList<? extends NewDependingOn>

    variable conditions used to express that a termsv depends on the free variables of a given formula(SV) Used by skolemization rules.

    varsNotFreeIn
    variables with a "x not free in y" variable condition.

  • Field Details

    • tacletAnnotations

      protected final ImmutableSet<TacletAnnotation> tacletAnnotations
      Annotations attached to taclets, specifying their kind, e.g., lemma.

    • name

      protected final Name name
      unique name of the taclet

    • displayName

      protected final String displayName
      name displayed by the pretty printer

    • find

      protected final @Nullable SyntaxElement find
      contains the find term

    • applicationRestriction

      protected final @NonNull ApplicationRestriction applicationRestriction
      The restriction(s) for applying this update. ApplicationRestriction.

    • assumesSequent

      protected final Sequent assumesSequent
      the assumes sequent of the taclet

    • varsNew

      protected final ImmutableList<? extends NewVarcond> varsNew
      Variables that have to be created each time the taclet is applied. Those variables occur in the varcond part of a taclet description.

    • varsNotFreeIn

      protected final ImmutableList<? extends NotFreeIn> varsNotFreeIn
      variables with a "x not free in y" variable condition. This means the instantiation of VariableSV x must not occur free in the instantiation of TermSV y.

    • varsNewDependingOn

      protected final ImmutableList<? extends NewDependingOn> varsNewDependingOn
      variable conditions used to express that a termsv depends on the free variables of a given formula(SV) Used by skolemization rules.

    • variableConditions

      protected final ImmutableList<? extends VariableCondition> variableConditions
      Additional generic conditions for schema variable instantiations.

    • goalTemplates

      protected final ImmutableList<TacletGoalTemplate> goalTemplates
      the list of taclet goal descriptions

    • choices

      protected final ChoiceExpr choices
      the set of taclet options for this taclet

    • prefixMap

      protected final ImmutableMap<@NonNull SchemaVariable, TacletPrefix> prefixMap
      map from a schemavariable to its prefix. The prefix is used to test correct instantiations of the schemavariables by resolving/avoiding collisions. Mainly the prefix consists of a list of all variables that may appear free in the instantiation of the schemavariable (a bit more complicated for rewrite taclets, see paper of M:Giese)

    • boundVariables

      protected @Nullable ImmutableSet<QuantifiableVariable> boundVariables
      cache; contains set of all bound variables

    • tacletAsString

      protected @Nullable String tacletAsString

    • assumesVariables

      protected @Nullable ImmutableSet<SchemaVariable> assumesVariables
      Set of schema variables of the assumes part

    • ruleSets

      protected final ImmutableList<RuleSet> ruleSets
      list of rulesets (formerly known as heuristics) the taclet belongs to

    • trigger

      protected final @Nullable Trigger trigger
      trigger of the taclet

    • matcher

      protected @NonNull TacletMatcher matcher
      The taclet matcher

    • executor

      protected @NonNull TacletExecutor<? extends @NonNull ProofGoal<?>, ? extends @NonNull RuleApp> executor
      The taclet executor

  • Constructor Details

    • Taclet

      @EnsuresNonNull({"matcher","executor"}) protected Taclet(Name name, SyntaxElement find, TacletApplPart applPart, ImmutableList<TacletGoalTemplate> goalTemplates, ImmutableList<RuleSet> ruleSets, TacletAttributes attrs, ImmutableMap<@NonNull SchemaVariable, TacletPrefix> prefixMap, ChoiceExpr choices, ImmutableSet<TacletAnnotation> tacletAnnotations)
      creates a Taclet (originally known as Schematic Theory Specific Rules)
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the Taclet
      find - the Term or Sequent that is the pattern that has to be found in a sequent and the places where it matches the Taclet can be applied
      applPart - contains the application part of a Taclet that is the if-sequence, the variable conditions
      goalTemplates - a list of goal descriptions.
      attrs - attributes for the Taclet; these are boolean values indicating a noninteractive or recursive use of the Taclet.

  • Method Details

    • hasTrigger

      public boolean hasTrigger()
      Returns:
      Whether the taclet has a trigger.

    • getTrigger

      public @Nullable Trigger getTrigger()

    • getMatcher

      @RequiresCalledMethods(value="this", methods="createAndInitializeMatcher") public final TacletMatcher getMatcher()

    • createAndInitializeMatcher

      @EnsuresNonNull("matcher") protected abstract void createAndInitializeMatcher(@UnderInitialization Taclet this)
      A helper method for creating the appropriate taclet matcher.

    • createAndInitializeExecutor

      @EnsuresNonNull("executor") protected abstract void createAndInitializeExecutor(@UnderInitialization Taclet this)
      A helper method for creating the appropriate taclet executor.

    • getBoundVariables

      public abstract ImmutableSet<QuantifiableVariable> getBoundVariables()
      computes and returns all variables that occur bound in the taclet including the taclets defined in addrules sections. The result is cached and therefore only computed once.
      Returns:
      all variables occurring bound in the taclet

    • getBoundVariablesHelper

      protected abstract ImmutableSet<QuantifiableVariable> getBoundVariablesHelper()
      collects bound variables in taclet entities others than goal templates
      Returns:
      set of variables that occur bound in taclet entities others than goal templates

    • closeGoal

      public boolean closeGoal()
      returns true iff the taclet contains a "close goal"-statement
      Returns:
      true iff the taclet contains a "close goal"-statement

    • varDeclaredNew

      public @Nullable NewVarcond varDeclaredNew(SchemaVariable var)
      looks if a variable is declared as new and returns its sort to match with or the schema variable it shares the match-sort with. Returns null if the SV is not declared to as new.
      Parameters:
      var - the SchemaVariable to look for
      Returns:
      the sort of the SV to match or the SV it shares the same match-sort with

    • getVariableConditions

      public ImmutableList<? extends VariableCondition> getVariableConditions()
      Returns:
      the generic variable conditions of this taclet

    • name

      public @NonNull Name name()
      Description copied from interface: Named
      returns the name of this element
      Specified by:
      name in interface Named
      Returns:
      the name of the Taclet

    • displayName

      public @NonNull String displayName()
      Description copied from interface: Rule
      returns the display name of the rule by default the name is the same as the rules unique name
      Specified by:
      displayName in interface Rule
      Returns:
      the display name of the taclet, or, if not specified -- the canonical name

    • assumesSequent

      public Sequent assumesSequent()
      Returns:
      the assumes-sequence of the application part of the Taclet.

    • applicationRestriction

      public ApplicationRestriction applicationRestriction()
      Returns:
      the application restrictions of the Taclet.

    • varsNew

      public ImmutableList<? extends NewVarcond> varsNew()
      Returns:
      an iterator over the variables that are new in the Taclet.

    • varsNotFreeIn

      public ImmutableList<? extends NotFreeIn> varsNotFreeIn()
      Returns:
      an iterator over the variable pairs that indicate that are new in the Taclet.

    • varsNewDependingOn

      public ImmutableList<? extends NewDependingOn> varsNewDependingOn()

    • goalTemplates

      public ImmutableList<TacletGoalTemplate> goalTemplates()
      Returns:
      an iterator over the goal descriptions.

    • prefixMap

      public ImmutableMap<@NonNull SchemaVariable, TacletPrefix> prefixMap()

    • hasReplaceWith

      public boolean hasReplaceWith()
      Returns:
      true if one of the goal templates is a replacewith. Already computed and cached by method cacheMatchInfo

    • getPrefix

      public @Nullable TacletPrefix getPrefix(SchemaVariable sv)
      Returns the computed prefix for the given schemavariable. The prefix of a schemavariable is used to determine if an instantiation is correct, in more detail: it mainly contains all variables that can appear free in an instantiation of the schemvariable sv (rewrite taclets have some special handling, see paper of M. Giese and comment of method isContextInPrefix).
      Parameters:
      sv - the Schemavariable
      Returns:
      prefix of schema variable sv

    • getChoices

      public ChoiceExpr getChoices()

    • ruleSets

      public Iterator<RuleSet> ruleSets()
      Specified by:
      ruleSets in interface Rule
      Returns:
      an iterator over the rule sets.

    • getAssumesVariables

      protected abstract ImmutableSet<? extends SchemaVariable> getAssumesVariables()
      Returns the set of schemavariables of the taclet's assumes-part
      Returns:
      Set of schemavariables of the if part

    • ignoreTopLevelUpdates

      public boolean ignoreTopLevelUpdates()
      This method is used to determine if top level updates are allowed to be ignored. This is the case if we have an AntecTaclet or SuccTaclet but not for a RewriteTaclet
      Returns:
      true if top level updates shall be ignored

    • isContextInPrefix

      public boolean isContextInPrefix()
      Returns true iff a context flag is set in one of the entries in the prefix map. Is cached after having been called once. OPTIMIZE is caching really necessary here?
      Returns:
      true iff a context flag is set in one of the entries in the prefix map.

    • getAssumesAndFindVariables

      public abstract ImmutableSet<SchemaVariable> getAssumesAndFindVariables()
      Returns:
      set of schemavariables of the \assumes and the (optional) \find part

      protected StringBuffer toStringAssumes(StringBuffer sb)
      Helper for toString(), specifically the assumes part.

      protected StringBuffer toStringVarCond(StringBuffer sb)
      Helper for toString(), specifically the variable conditions.

      Helper for toString(), specifically the goal templates.

      Helper for toString(), specifically the triggers.

      Helper for toString(), specifically the rule sets.

      Returns a representation of the Taclet as String
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      string representation

    • getExecutor

      public <G extends ProofGoal<@NonNull G>> @NonNull TacletExecutor<@NonNull G, ?> getExecutor()
      Description copied from interface: Rule
      Returns the rule executor for this rule. The rule executor encapsulates the logic for rule applications.
      Specified by:
      getExecutor in interface Rule
      Type Parameters:
      G - kind of goal on which the executor operates
      Returns:
      the rule executor for this rule

    • setName

      public abstract Taclet setName(String name)
